On this day in history, 16 years ago, Satoshi Nakamoto explained that mining more and more Bitcoin transactions “does NOT slow down your generation rate. When generate is scanning hashes, it only hashes the header of the block, which is a constant size.”
As of last night, new bitcoins are 10.09% easier to mine as the network programmatically adjusted difficulty at block 953,568, marking the 11th-largest automatic decreased change in history.
